rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HENRIETTA WEBBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/27/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The conservatorship estate cannot be closed/transferred until a personal representative is appointed. (2) Exhibit A does not appear to identify the financial institution and does not appear to be complete (not dated or signed). Also, it appears to be the conservator's signature card, not the conservatee's. Was there a previous signature card executed by the conservatee, designating the beneficiary? (3) Item 4 of the proof of service was incomplete (although there is apparently no one to whom notice is to be mailed at this time). Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/16/24. If no personal representative has been appointed in time to give sufficient notice to that person of the 8/27/24 hearing, Counsel should request a further continuance from the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISA THEADORA MARIA VAN VELZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/16/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) Each status report must allege the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) including allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution, even if the same information was alleged in the previous report. 2) Did the petitioner take any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a) during this second status report period? 3) The decedent died more than two years ago, but no first interim accounting covering a two-year period from the date of death has been filed. Does the petitioner anticipate obtaining waivers of account from all the beneficiaries or applying for the Court's approval to exceed the account period under LRSF 14.34(A)? 4) Please provide a proof that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). 5) Louis Gerald Hendrikus van Velzen's address listed on the proof of service is different from his address previously reported. Has he moved? 6) Re Marie Nieuweboer: i) Please provide her date of death. ii) Please provide a copy of letters issued to Aad Meijer (or any other person) as personal representative of her estate. iii) If there is no court-appointed personal representative of her estate, please identify (names and address) all successors in interest who intend to sign small estate affidavits.

Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If notice of the hearing scheduled for 5/20/24 was not given in compliance with LRSF 14.14 A., how have all parties now been so served? Probate Code Section 1205 only applies if notice given for the hearing that was continued was sufficient. (2) The summary of account shall be supported by detailed schedules showing the following: (a) Receipts, showing the nature or purpose of each item, the source of the receipt, and the date thereof. [Probate Code Section 1062(a).] Receipts not so set forth cannot be included in the statutory fee base calculation. (3) Nothing has been filed to confirm that a personal representative has been appointed for Felicia Williams's estate (i.e., certified copy of Letters certified no more than 60 days before the hearing). Did the Letters of Special Administration issued to Armani Cooper even show that she is authorized to marshal assets? [LRSF 14.35 G.] (4) Far from the bank records "clearly" indicating "each item requested for reimbursement," the truncated and redacted copies of the Bank of America statements are not at all supportive. (5) The records indicate that Administrator sold real property under his IAEA authority. [CRC 7.250.] Deadline is 7/29/24 for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive papers. A personal representative should sign and verify HIS own supplements or amendments to his pleadings, not merely verify his attorney's declarations. [CRC 7.103.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (An "amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3).] Relying on prior supplements filed to address issues in a petition that was amended is inadequate. A petition for final distribution is not a petition "to probate" anything)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA OSALVO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: (1) There have been two First Amended Petitions on file. 1/22/2024 and 1/26/2024. Need clarification if the later document must be the "Second" Amended Petition. (2) Proposed personal representative appears to be Administrator with Will Annexed not Executor because it is unknown if the purported lost will named himself as executor. (3) A copy of the lost will to be provided. It needs to be attached to Petition. Also, a copy of the lost will need to be submitted to the clerk's office for the purpose of admission. (4) Need allegation on the lost will pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 8223. Attachment 3F(3) alleges the will was not in the decedent's possession; however, petition does not provide any supportive facts or proof. (5) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate needs to be served on all persons titled. Prob. Code sec. 8110. The notice filed on 1/22/2024; yet it does not include the notice of direction of remote appearance. LRSF 14.1.C.6) (6) Petition to suggest an appropriate bond amount of $1,100,000. First Amended Petition filed 1/22/2024 requests $100,000 bond be ordered (#2.d.(2)); however, full bond of $1,100,000 is required. Prob. Code sec. 8482(a). If Petition seeks no IAEA, the court may require a minimum bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUIS D. VIDALON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: 1. Notice of Hearing [DE-120] to the children. See Prob. Code sec. 13655. Notice on file shows Coinbase was noticed, not the children. 2. Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access. See Local Rule 14.1.C.6). Form RA-010 was filed, which is insufficient. The Local Rule instructs appropriate notice in detail. 3. Proposed Spousal Property Order [Form DE-226]. See Local Rule 14.7. The order was submitted; however, #5-#7 do not mirror the relief in petition. Also, Proposed order include 8 pages of attachment of the decedent's will. The info should have been separately filed before the hearing. Attaching to proposed order inappropriate. 4. Petition itself instructs an attachment to be filed; however, no such attachments to the petition have been filed. Attachment #7.a. describing the specific property subject to the request. See #7.a. 5. Attachment #7 setting forth supporting facts upon which the petitioner bases the allegation that the property identified in Attachment #7.a. See #7. Also, see Local Rule 14.26 in detail. 6. Attachment #7.b if Petition #1.b intentionally affirms what Petitioner seeks. See Prob. Code sec. 13650 and 13651 in detail. 7. Attachment #11 as petition affirms that the decedent died testate (with a will). If the allegation is accurate, Petition#4.c must mirror the information, too. 8. Also, need clarification if the decedent left a pour-over will, leaving all assets to a trust. If so, this petition seems inappropriate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLOS NELSON MONTOYA JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/5/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 2d of the petition must be answered. (2) Item 5a(2)(a) or (b) of the petition must be answered. (3) If the petitioner is acting in her capacity as the agent for the decedent's mother, a copy of the durable power of attorney must be filed. (4) A proposed personal representative must sign and verify all of her own pleadings, including any supplement to a petition. [CRC 7.103(c).] (5) Notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
